scope.branch: feature/T-016-m2-closeout-and-treehouse-dogfooding (fast-forwarded into main; deleted per the branching strategy rule 7)
in_scope: Introduce the Milestone Closeout Standard at .ai/workflows/milestone-closeout.md (10 sections; the canonical procedure every future milestone closeout must follow; the 13-section retrospective is the standard's required deliverable).

Generate the M2 retrospective at retrospective-m2-application-shell-and-navigation.md (13 sections: delivered capabilities, deferred capabilities, technical debt, known issues, lessons learned, architecture changes, documentation changes, testing summary, validation results, implementation reports, commit range, readiness for M3, recommendations for the next milestone).

Validate the complete M2 milestone end-to-end: npm run css:build (exit 0), dotnet restore (exit 0), dotnet build (0 warnings, 0 errors), dotnet test (197 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped per ADR-016), dotnet format --verify-no-changes (exit 0), visual smoke (5 routes return 200 on localhost:5211; theme toggle present on every AppLayout page).

Update the project-continuity state per Rule 15: session.json, tasks.json (T-016 Done; T-006 M2 summary Done; T-018 M3.1 promoted to Ready; T-007 M3 summary note updated), current.md (active milestone M2 closed; last completed task T-016; active branch main; last stable commit the M2 closeout commit; active plan status M3 Awaiting Approval; next recommended task T-018; linked artefacts updated), task-board.md (In Progress block empty; M2.6 added to Done Recently; M3 promoted to Ready), milestones.json (M2.6 from in_progress to delivered; M2 from Active to Done with closed_at 2026-07-11; M2 evidence block updated with M2.6 handoff + report + retrospective + slice entry; M3 plan path added), ROADMAP.md (§ 2 M2 row Done; § 3 M2.6 row Delivered; M2 DoD expanded), .ai/plans/master-delivery-plan.md (§ 1 M2 row Done; § 3 M2 completion status Done; M2 evidence list updated; M2.6 slice row Delivered), .ai/handoffs/latest.md (mirror of the M2.6 handoff).
Write the M2.6 implementation report at implementation-report-m2-6-m2-closeout.md and the M2.6 per-session handoff at .ai/handoffs/2026-07-11-m2-6-m2-closeout.md.
Create the focused M2 closeout commit on the feature branch: chore(m2.6): close M2 with retrospective, milestone closeout standard, and M3 plan.
Fast-forward merge the M2.6 feature branch into main per the branching strategy rule 6.
Delete the M2.6 feature branch per the branching strategy rule 7.
Create the m2 annotated milestone tag at the M2 closeout commit on main per the branching strategy rule 9.
Prepare the M3 plan at .ai/plans/M3-project-registration.md with Status: Awaiting Approval; promote the first M3 task (T-018 — M3.1) to Ready in tasks.json.
out_of_scope: M3 implementation. The brief explicitly says 'do not expand into M3 implementation' and 'Do not begin M3 implementation.' M2.6 ends with the M3 plan in Awaiting Approval and the first M3 task in Ready; the M3 implementation lands in the next session.

Pushing to the remote. The brief says 'Push only if a remote is configured and pushing is authorised.' The remote (origin = https://github.com/maestroohk/ai-engineering-platform.git) is configured but pushing is not authorised in this session; the prior session's rule (push requires explicit user authorisation per the command protocol) is preserved. Push is deferred to a follow-up user command.

Renaming legacy feature branches. The M0.5 → M2.5 feature branches use the older feature/<milestone>-<short-kebab> naming. They remain as historical record per .ai/workflows/branching-strategy.md § 6.

The lavish-axi M1 design-system review. The tool is not installed on the host; the review is Blocked in .ai/state/task-board.md and the T-005 entry of .ai/state/tasks.json. The M2 retrospective records the deferral. The M3 plan does not assume the review lands.

The Treehouse M2 dogfooding checkpoint. The M2.6 slice records the checkpoint in the M2 retrospective's § 4 Known Issues; the M2 milestone is closed with the checkpoint deferred to a future session (the user may exercise the dogfooding externally at any time).

last_action.outcome: M2 is closed 2026-07-11. The Milestone Closeout Standard is at .ai/workflows/milestone-closeout.md (10 sections; canonical procedure every future milestone closeout must follow; the 13-section retrospective is the standard's required deliverable; the standard is the single source of truth for milestone closeout procedures). The M2 retrospective is at retrospective-m2-application-shell-and-navigation.md (13 sections, all populated; the first milestone retrospective in the repository). The M2.6 implementation report is at implementation-report-m2-6-m2-closeout.md. The M2.6 per-session handoff is at .ai/handoffs/2026-07-11-m2-6-m2-closeout.md (mirrored to latest.md). The M3 plan is at .ai/plans/M3-project-registration.md (Status: Awaiting Approval). The first M3 task (T-018 — M3.1) is Ready in tasks.json. The M2 closeout commit is on main; the m2 annotated milestone tag is at the M2 closeout commit on main; the M2.6 feature branch is deleted per the branching strategy. Validation results: 197 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped (per ADR-016); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke green. No push (not authorised).

intended_next_action: Stop. The next session is the M3 plan approval and M3.1 implementation. The M3 plan is in Awaiting Approval; the first M3 task (T-018) is in Ready. Do NOT begin M3 implementation in this session.
session_notes: M2 closeout session (2026-07-11). The user's brief was to (1) reconcile Git, structured state, the roadmap, the task board, the latest handoff, and the M2 milestone; (2) confirm M2.6 is the first dependency-satisfied Ready task; (3) mark M2.6 In Progress; (4) implement M2.6 within planned scope; (5) complete the M2 milestone; (6) not expand into M3 implementation; (7) introduce the Milestone Closeout Standard; (8) make milestone retrospectives a required deliverable; (9) generate the M2 retrospective with 13 sections; (10) validate the complete M2 milestone (npm run css:build, dotnet restore, dotnet build, dotnet test, dotnet format --verify-no-changes, visual smoke); (11) update capability evidence, milestone evidence, task state, session state, structured JSON state, markdown projections, ROADMAP.md, master delivery plan, current state, task board, latest handoff, milestone history, implementation report, retrospective; (12) review the complete diff before committing; (13) create a focused M2 closeout commit; (14) merge the M2 work into main; (15) create the m2 milestone tag; (16) push only if remote is configured and pushing is authorised; (17) prepare the M3 plan in .ai/plans/ with Status: Awaiting Approval; (18) promote the first M3 task to Ready; (19) stop. The session executes the brief in the order: (a) reconcile state with the repository; (b) introduce the Milestone Closeout Standard at .ai/workflows/milestone-closeout.md; (c) generate the M2 retrospective at retrospective-m2-application-shell-and-navigation.md (13 sections); (d) validate the complete M2 milestone end-to-end (6 gates, all green); (e) update the project-continuity state per Rule 15 (session.json, tasks.json, current.md, task-board.md, milestones.json, ROADMAP.md, master-delivery-plan.md, handoffs/latest.md); (f) write the M2.6 implementation report at implementation-report-m2-6-m2-closeout.md; (g) write the M2.6 per-session handoff at .ai/handoffs/2026-07-11-m2-6-m2-closeout.md (mirrored to latest.md); (h) prepare the M3 plan at .ai/plans/M3-project-registration.md (Status: Awaiting Approval); (i) promote the first M3 task (T-018 — M3.1) to Ready in tasks.json; (j) create the single coherent commit on the feature branch (chore(m2.6): close M2 with retrospective, milestone closeout standard, and M3 plan); (k) fast-forward the feature branch into main per the branching strategy rule 6; (l) delete the feature branch per rule 7; (m) create the m2 annotated milestone tag at the M2 closeout commit on main per rule 9; (n) stop.
